QF.id: XIII Introduction to the Underlying Markets_63

question.text: Which of the following statements correctly compares OTC and exchange-traded derivatives across all asset classes?

answer.text: OTC derivatives offer customization and are bilaterally negotiated; exchange-traded derivatives are standardized with central counterparty guarantee but less flexible
OTC derivatives are safer because they are regulated by the central bank; exchange-traded derivatives are unregulated
OTC derivatives have daily mark-to-market; exchange-traded derivatives settle only at maturity
OTC derivatives are only available for currencies; exchange-traded derivatives cover all asset classes

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Across all asset classes, OTC derivatives offer customization (notional, maturity, terms) but carry counterparty risk. Exchange-traded derivatives are standardized (lot size, expiry) with CCP guarantee and daily MTM. The trade-off is flexibility vs safety/liquidity. **The Trap:** Exchange-traded derivatives are well-regulated. Daily MTM is a feature of exchange-traded (not OTC) products. OTC derivatives exist for all asset classes. **Cross-Exam Context:** This fundamental distinction is covered in Series VIII Ch1, Series I Ch2, Series IV Ch2, and Series XVI Ch1. It's a core concept for Series XIII. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Post-2008 reforms have pushed many OTC derivatives toward central clearing — the line between OTC and exchange-traded is blurring with mandatory clearing requirements.
